title: podman run --detach --rm doesn't remove the container (rootless)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

As a non-root user: `podman run --rm --detach busybox:latest /bin/sleep 5`

**Describe the results you received:**

    $ ps -e | grep conmon
    $
    $ podman ps -a
    CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                             COMMAND       CREATED             STATUS                         PORTS       NAMES
    a2d3b39df788  docker.io/library/busybox:latest  /bin/sleep 5  About a minute ago  Exited (0) About a minute ago              happy_shirley

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container should have been removed.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.3
API Version:  4.0.3
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Thu Apr  7 02:00:00 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-4.0.3-1.1.x86_64
```
(openSUSE Tumbleweed)

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

rootless

comments.body: Usually indicates an error in the cleanup process. Can you add `--log-level=debug` to your `podman run` command, then search syslog (either `journalctl` or `/var/log/messages` depending on whether your system uses journald) for any messages from Conmon, and post those?

(Specifically, for messages from Conmon after the container should have been removed - assuming `sleep 5` as the command, give it maybe 10 seconds?)

Also, any messages from Podman would also help.

Hmm, there's not a lot in the logs. The only thing that stans out is `Using volume path /mnt/vms/_NOBACKUP_/podman/volumes`, since  `volume_path` is actually `/home/lukas/lxcshare`. Also, running podman without `--detach` works just fine.

**podman**

We are invoking the cleanup process with `--rm` so Podman is being instructed to remove the container. The 2681 podman process seems to be cleanup, and it's not doing anything, which is concerning. Can you see if you have any Podman processes hanging in the background? If not, I wonder if something is killing `podman container cleanup` before it can succeed.

Okay, I was able to strace the the podman cleanup process, and it looks like it wanted to log an additional error message that got swallowed somehow. Look at the bottom. Also, I attached my configs from `~/.config/containers`

```

Could you use the driver "overlay" instead of btrfs and see if this works properly.  Is this on an SELinux system?  If so did you fix your labeling?

The volume_path is likely to be the issue though.  WDYT @mheon
It fails the same way with the overlay storage driver. This system doesn't use SELinux.

Core issue: the cleanup process is being executed with an explicit `--root` and `--runroot` argument. These are overriding `volume_path` by default. Because they're passed as explicit arguments, and the DB says the actual volume_path is different, Podman refuses to run as there's a configuration mismatch - we can't safely execute with a different DB path.

Solution: We need to add a `--volume-path` flag to the global Podman command, and pass it to the cleanup process with appropriate value, to ensure the volume path is set appropriately and won't be overridden by `--root`
@mheon Are you going to add this?
Earliest I can get to this is mid-to-late next week. If anyone wants to take this before then, they can have it; otherwise I'll do it then.
